$schema: Target output schema — one object per interaction
_notes: Not every field will be extractable from every interaction. Use null for fields that cannot be reliably determined. Arrays can be empty if nothing applies. The schema represents the ideal production output.

interaction.type: enum: sales_inquiry | support_request | cancellation | upgrade_inquiry | general_inquiry | complaint | win_back
interaction.duration_seconds: integer | null — extract from interaction metadata if available

agent.name: string
agent.handled_well: boolean — did the agent handle the interaction effectively?
agent.notable_actions: string | null — anything the agent did particularly well or poorly

resolution.status: enum: resolved | partially_resolved | unresolved | escalated | cancelled
resolution.summary: string — one-sentence summary of the outcome

customer.name: string | null
customer.tenure_months: integer | null — infer from conversation if mentioned
customer.current_plan: enum: free_trial | basic | premium | family | enterprise | unknown
customer.lifecycle_stage: enum: new | onboarding | active | at_risk | churning | churned | win_back

demographic_signals.household: string | null — e.g. 'family with kids', 'lives alone', 'couple'

sentiment.overall: enum: very_negative | negative | mixed | neutral | positive | very_positive
sentiment.trajectory: enum: improving | stable | declining — how sentiment changed during the interaction
sentiment.emotional_intensity: enum: low | moderate | high — how strongly the customer expressed emotion

key_moments.timestamp: string | null — approximate time in interaction (format varies: phone uses elapsed time like 00:02:18, chat uses wall-clock time like 14:22:08)

pain_points.verbatim_quote: string — direct quote from the customer
pain_points.actionable: boolean — is this something the company can realistically fix?

competitor_mentions.name: string — competitor name
competitor_mentions.context: enum: currently_using | considering | switched_to | switched_from | comparing
competitor_mentions.detail: string — what specifically was compared or mentioned

intent.primary: string — the main reason for the interaction

churn_risk.level: enum: none | low | medium | high | immediate

upsell_opportunity.target_plan: string | null — what plan/feature they might upgrade to
signals: string — what suggests they might upgrade

action_items.owner: enum: agent | customer | engineering | billing | product | management

quality_flags.interaction_quality: enum: clean | minor_issues | significant_gaps — quality of the source interaction data
quality_flags.extraction_confidence: enum: high | medium | low — how confident are you in the extracted data?
review_reasons: string — why human review is needed; empty array if not needed
